examinations.default_action: Begin systematic cardiovascular assessment starting with symptom history, then precordium inspection, palpation, and auscultation.

questions.text: What cardiac symptoms does the patient report?

options: Described as tight band or heaviness
Not relieved by rest or nitrates
Peripheral cyanosis (nails / nose / ear lobules only)
Relative bradycardia (slower than expected for fever)
Collapsing / water-hammer pulse (high volume, rapid downstroke)
Apex beat in normal position (5th ICS, medial to MCL)
Murmur at Mitral area (5th ICS MCL)
Murmur at Tricuspid area (lower left sternal border)
Murmur at Aortic area (2nd ICS right sternal border)
Murmur at Pulmonary area (2nd ICS left sternal border)

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — Pulsus paradoxus detected. Assess for cardiac tamponade signs:
FOLLOWUP — PND reported. Assess for left heart failure features:
options: Patient wakes at night relieved by sitting upright

value: Murmur at Mitral area (5th ICS MCL)
Murmur at Tricuspid area (lower left sternal border)
Murmur at Aortic area (2nd ICS right sternal border)
Murmur at Pulmonary area (2nd ICS left sternal border)

action.message: Murmur detected — characterize further.
Pulsus paradoxus — assess for cardiac tamponade.
PND reported — assess left heart failure.

conditions.value: Not relieved by rest or nitrates

action.message: HIGH PRIORITY: Pattern consistent with Myocardial Infarction. Urgent assessment required.

diagnoses.name: Cardiac Tamponade
diagnoses.description: Life-threatening compression of the heart by pericardial fluid. Classic Beck triad: muffled heart sounds + raised JVP + hypotension. Pulsus paradoxus is the hallmark sign on pulse examination.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Pulsus paradoxus(3)+tachycardia(1) | raised JVP(3)+muffled sounds(3)+hypotension(3) | muffled heart sounds(3) = 16

diagnoses.name: Myocardial Infarction
diagnoses.description: Acute coronary occlusion causing myocardial necrosis. Chest pain lasting >30 minutes, not relieved by rest or sublingual nitrates. May radiate to arm/jaw/back. Requires emergency reperfusion.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Chest pain(2)+dyspnea(1) | duration>30min(3)+not relieved(3)+radiation(2)+retrosternal(1) = 12

diagnoses.name: Left Heart Failure
diagnoses.description: Failure of the left ventricle to adequately pump blood. Key features: PND, orthopnea, displaced apex beat, S3 gallop, basal crepitations, raised JVP and peripheral edema in combined failure.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Symptoms(7) | LHF signs(16) | GPE edema+JVP(4) | displaced apex+LV heave(4) = 29 — broad multi-system presentation

diagnoses.name: Atrial Fibrillation
diagnoses.description: Chaotic atrial electrical activity producing an irregularly irregular pulse with variable rate. Pulse deficit (radial rate < auscultated rate) is characteristic. Risk of thromboembolic stroke.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Irregularly irregular(2)+pulse deficit(2)+tachycardia(1) = 5 — single-fact rhythm diagnosis

diagnoses.name: Aortic Stenosis
diagnoses.description: Obstruction of LV outflow across a calcified/bicuspid aortic valve. Ejection systolic murmur in aortic area radiating to carotids, with left ventricular heave and slow-rising pulse. Classic triad: syncope, angina, dyspnea.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Aortic area murmur(2) | ejection systolic(1)+carotid radiation(1) | LV heave(2)+thrill(2) = 8

examinations.default_action: Begin respiratory assessment with symptom history, then systematic inspection, palpation, percussion, and auscultation of the chest.

questions.text: What respiratory symptoms does the patient report?
options: Chest pain (pleuritic — sharp, worse on breathing)
Reduced / absent breath sounds on RIGHT
Reduced / absent breath sounds on LEFT

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — Bronchial breathing detected. Assess for consolidation (Pneumonia):
options: Reduced chest expansion on affected side

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — Stony dull percussion note. Assess for Pleural Effusion:
options: Reduced chest expansion on affected side

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — Hyper-resonant percussion note. Assess for Pneumothorax:
options: Absent breath sounds on affected side
Reduced chest expansion on affected side

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — Stridor reported. Assess for upper airway obstruction:

action.message: Bronchial breathing detected — assess for consolidation/pneumonia.
Stony dullness — assess for pleural effusion.
Hyper-resonance — assess for pneumothorax.
Stridor present — assess upper airway urgently.

value: Reduced / absent breath sounds on RIGHT
Reduced / absent breath sounds on LEFT

action.message: HIGH PRIORITY: Triad of tracheal deviation + hyper-resonance + absent breath sounds — TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX. Emergency intervention required.

diagnoses.name: Tension Pneumothorax
diagnoses.description: Air under pressure in the pleural space causing mediastinal shift. Life-threatening emergency. Triad: tracheal deviation AWAY from affected side + hyper-resonance + absent breath sounds. Immediate needle decompression required.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Trachea deviation+reduced expansion(4) | hyper-resonance(2) | absent sounds(2) | full pneumothorax followup(11) = 19

diagnoses.name: Upper Airway Obstruction
diagnoses.description: Obstruction above the carina causing inspiratory stridor. Causes include foreign body, epiglottitis, anaphylaxis, or extrinsic compression. Biphasic stridor and drooling indicate severe obstruction requiring urgent airway management.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Stridor symptom(3) | inspiratory+biphasic+drooling+accessory(10) | tachypnea+accessory muscles(4) = 17

diagnoses.name: Pleural Effusion
diagnoses.description: Fluid accumulation in the pleural space. Classic signs: stony dull percussion, absent/reduced breath sounds, tracheal deviation AWAY from effusion (large), and decreased vocal resonance. Underlying cause must be sought.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Stony dull(3) | absent sounds+decreased VR(3) | trachea+expansion(4) | effusion followup(9) = 19

diagnoses.name: Pneumonia / Consolidation
diagnoses.description: Infection causing alveolar consolidation. Classic signs: dull percussion, bronchial breathing, increased vocal resonance/aegophony, and crepitations over affected lobe. Rusty sputum is characteristic of pneumococcal pneumonia.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Symptoms(5) | rusty sputum(2) | dull percussion(2) | bronchial+creps(4) | consolidation followup(11) = 22 (typical confirmed pneumonia)

diagnoses.name: Asthma / COPD
diagnoses.description: Obstructive airway disease causing expiratory wheeze, dyspnea, and cough. Asthma is reversible; COPD features barrel chest from air trapping, accessory muscle use, and pursed-lip breathing. Both show bilateral rhonchi on auscultation.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Wheeze+dyspnea(3) | tachypnea+accessory+barrel(5) | rhonchi(1) = 9 — obstructive pattern is relatively low-weight

examinations.default_action: Begin abdominal assessment with symptom history, then systematic inspection, light palpation, deep palpation, percussion, and auscultation.

questions.text: What abdominal symptoms does the patient report?
Light palpation findings (start in right iliac fossa, move systematically):

options: Spleen palpable (splenomegaly — must be 2-3x normal size to feel)
History of liver disease / alcohol use

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — Signs of peritoneal irritation. Assess for peritonitis:
FOLLOWUP — Hematemesis / Melena reported. Assess upper GI bleed:
options: Hematemesis confirmed (bright red or coffee ground)
History of NSAID or aspirin use
Periodic burning epigastric pain relieved by food (duodenal ulcer pattern)

action.message: Ascites detected — full assessment.
Peritoneal signs present — urgent peritonitis assessment.
Upper GI bleed suspected — detailed assessment.
HIGH PRIORITY: Classical peritonitis triad. Emergency surgical assessment required.

diagnoses.name: Peritonitis
diagnoses.description: Inflammation of the peritoneum, usually from bowel perforation or ruptured viscus. Classic signs: board-like rigid abdomen, generalized rebound tenderness, absent bowel sounds, and patient lying completely still. Surgical emergency.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Pain+vomiting(2) | guarding triad(9) | peritonitis signs fully positive(17) = ~25 for confirmed peritonitis with shock

diagnoses.name: Upper GI Bleed
diagnoses.description: Bleeding proximal to the ligament of Treitz. Presents as hematemesis (bright red or coffee-ground) and/or melena. Hemodynamic compromise (tachycardia + hypotension) indicates massive bleed. Most common cause: peptic ulcer disease.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Hematemesis+melena(6) | confirmed bleed+haemodynamic compromise+PUD+pallor(13) = ~16 typical massive UGI bleed

diagnoses.name: Liver Cirrhosis with Ascites
diagnoses.description: End-stage liver disease with portal hypertension leading to ascites. Features: shifting dullness/fluid thrill, splenomegaly, caput medusae (dilated abdominal veins), jaundice, and everted umbilicus. Hepatomegaly or shrunken liver may be found.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Symptoms(3)+inspection(5)+percussion(7)+organomegaly(4)+ascites followup(10 typical) = ~29 advanced cirrhosis

diagnoses.name: Appendicitis
diagnoses.description: Inflammation of the appendix. Classic migration of pain from periumbilical to right iliac fossa (McBurney's point). Tenderness + guarding in RIF, low-grade fever, and anorexia. Rebound tenderness at McBurney's point is highly suggestive.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Nausea+vomiting+pain(3) | RIF tenderness+guarding+rebound(8) | RIF+periumbilical pain character(2) = 13

diagnoses.name: Bowel Obstruction
diagnoses.description: Mechanical or functional obstruction of small or large bowel. Features: colicky abdominal pain, vomiting, abdominal distension, absolute constipation (no flatus), and visible peristalsis on inspection. High-pitched tinkling bowel sounds on auscultation.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Vomiting+constipation+distension(3) | distension+visible peristalsis(3) | colicky pain(1) | mild tenderness(2) = 9

examinations.default_action: Begin neurological assessment with higher mental functions and consciousness, then systematically assess cranial nerves, motor system, sensory system, reflexes, and coordination.

questions.text: Level of consciousness — Glasgow Coma Scale assessment:

options: Aphasia present (cannot understand or produce speech)
CN III/IV/VI — eye movements full (H-test normal)
CN III/IV/VI — restricted eye movements / diplopia
CN VII — facial weakness (upper / lower motor neuron)
CN VIII (Vestibulocochlear) — hearing normal (Rinne / Weber tests)
CN IX/X — palate moves symmetrically on saying Ah
CN IX/X — palatal palsy / absent gag reflex
CN XI — shoulder shrug and head turn normal
CN XI — weakness of trapezius / sternomastoid
CN XII — tongue central, no wasting or fasciculations
CN XII — tongue deviation / wasting / fasciculations
Plantar response extensor — Babinski Sign POSITIVE (UMN lesion)
Vibration sense normal (128 Hz tuning fork)
Neck rigidity PRESENT (resistance to passive flexion)
Kernig's Sign POSITIVE (cannot extend knee with hip flexed 90°)
Brudzinski's Sign POSITIVE (neck flexion causes hip/knee flexion)

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — UMN pattern detected. Localize the lesion:
options: Unilateral upper and lower limb involvement (same side) — Cortical / Capsular
Both lower limbs involved — Spinal cord lesion (Paraplegia)
All four limbs involved — High cervical cord or brainstem
Crossed signs (ipsilateral face + contralateral body) — Brainstem
Sensory level on trunk — Spinal cord level lesion

questions.text: FOLLOWUP — LMN pattern detected. Characterize further:
FOLLOWUP — Meningeal irritation signs present. Full meningitis assessment:
options: Classic triad: fever + headache + neck stiffness
CSF examination / lumbar puncture indicated (L3-L4 or L4-L5)

conditions.value: Plantar response extensor — Babinski Sign POSITIVE (UMN lesion)

action.message: UMN pattern identified — localize the lesion.
LMN pattern identified — characterize and localize.

value: Neck rigidity PRESENT (resistance to passive flexion)
Kernig's Sign POSITIVE (cannot extend knee with hip flexed 90°)
Brudzinski's Sign POSITIVE (neck flexion causes hip/knee flexion)

action.message: Meningeal irritation — assess for meningitis.

conditions.value: Neck rigidity PRESENT (resistance to passive flexion)
Kernig's Sign POSITIVE (cannot extend knee with hip flexed 90°)

action.message: HIGH PRIORITY: Meningitis triad confirmed (fever + neck rigidity + Kernig's positive). Urgent CSF analysis required. Check for papilledema before LP.
HIGH PRIORITY: Severely depressed level of consciousness. GCS scoring required. Airway, breathing, circulation — immediate assessment.

conditions.value: Aphasia present (cannot understand or produce speech)

action.message: Aphasia detected — differentiate Broca (expressive) vs Wernicke (receptive) vs Global aphasia. Consider stroke / mass lesion.
Sensory level detected — spinal cord lesion. Map dermatomes to determine level.

diagnoses.name: Meningitis
diagnoses.description: Infection/inflammation of the meninges. Classic triad: fever + severe headache + neck rigidity. Kernig's and Brudzinski's signs positive. Petechial rash suggests meningococcal disease. Lumbar puncture (L3-L4) is diagnostic; check for papilledema first to exclude raised ICP.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Full meningeal triad(13)+photophobia+fever(4) | classic triad+altered consciousness+petechiae(9) | confusion(2) = 24

diagnoses.name: Raised Intracranial Pressure
diagnoses.description: Elevated pressure within the cranial vault from mass, edema, or hydrocephalus. Features: progressively deteriorating consciousness, CN III palsy (ptosis + dilated pupil), papilledema on fundoscopy, bradycardia with hypertension (Cushing's reflex). LP is contraindicated.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Drowsy(2) | CN II+III visual/eye signs(6) | papilledema on followup(3) = 11 — focal signs without meningeal triad

diagnoses.name: Stroke / CVA
diagnoses.description: Acute focal neurological deficit from cerebral ischemia or hemorrhage. UMN pattern: contralateral hemiplegia, facial weakness, Babinski positive, brisk reflexes. Aphasia if dominant hemisphere affected. Sudden onset is characteristic. Requires urgent imaging.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Hemiplegia+spasticity(5) | aphasia(3) | CN VII(2) | Babinski+brisk reflexes(5) | UMN localization(2) = 17

diagnoses.name: Spinal Cord Compression
diagnoses.description: Compression of the spinal cord by disc prolapse, tumor, or abscess. Bilateral UMN signs below level of lesion: paraplegia/tetraplegia, spasticity, brisk reflexes, Babinski positive, and a clear sensory level on the trunk. Bladder and bowel dysfunction common. Neurosurgical emergency.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Paraplegia+spasticity+reduced power(7) | sensory level+JPS+vibration(7) | Babinski+brisk reflexes(7) | spinal localisation(6) = 27

diagnoses.name: Peripheral Neuropathy
diagnoses.description: Damage to peripheral nerves producing LMN pattern: distal muscle wasting, fasciculations, flaccidity, absent/reduced deep tendon reflexes (especially ankle jerks), and glove-and-stocking sensory loss. Vibration and joint position sense impaired. Common causes: diabetes, alcohol, B12 deficiency.
diagnoses.score_rationale: Wasting+fasciculations+flaccidity(6) | vibration+JPS(4) | absent ankle+knee jerks(4) | LMN localization typical(10) = ~24

per_question_score_interpretation.3: Severe / high-risk finding — urgent attention required

rule_operators_reference.contains: The stored array includes this exact value
rule_operators_reference.contains_any: The stored array includes at least one value from the given list
rule_operators_reference.contains_all: The stored array includes all values in the given list
rule_operators_reference.equals: The stored scalar equals this value
rule_operators_reference.greater_than: The stored numeric value exceeds this threshold
